notes: z19 best balances house, driveway, road, and wooded lot/backyard context; z20 is tight/soft and z18 is broader. Tree cover obscures parts of the lot. Google quality is average to slightly worse than typical Mapbox/Bing high-res sheets due to softness/shadow/tree obstruction.

Clear top-down suburban view; z19 shows house, front drive, backyard/lot, and neighboring context. z20 is usable but tighter. Google quality is good and likely comparable to or slightly better than typical Mapbox/Bing.
z19 clearly captures the house, lot, yard, street corner/context; z20 is close but still somewhat useful. Google quality is good/comparable to typical Mapbox/Bing, with minor roof/shadow blur.

Useful for lot and construction footprint/foundation, but not a finished house; z19 shows the build area, road, and lot context best. z18 adds neighborhood/parcel context. Google quality is clear and likely better than typical Mapbox/Bing for this developing area.

z19 gives the best complete view of house, driveway, backyard/pool area, and lot context; z20 is detailed but tighter. Google quality is good and comparable to or slightly better than typical Mapbox/Bing.

z19 shows the house, driveway, lawn/backyard area, and surrounding lot context together; z20 is sharper but too cropped. Google quality is clear and likely better than typical Mapbox/Bing for this sheet.
z19 gives a strong top-down view of house, yard, driveway, and lot edges; z20 is detailed but more cropped. Google quality is sharp and likely better than typical Mapbox/Bing.
Coordinate appears to fall on an undeveloped/vacant lot beside the road; no subject house/backyard is visible. Google imagery is clear, but not useful for house+lot assessment.
Subject appears to be a vacant corner lot with no house present; z18/z19 provide context only. Google quality is good, but coverage does not satisfy house+backyard/lot need.

z19 shows house and yard/lot together, but shadows, trees, and slightly soft imagery reduce interpretability; z20 is too cropped/blurry. Google quality appears worse than the clearer Whiskey Jack/Black Hawk sheets and may be only comparable to or worse than typical Mapbox/Bing.
